metadata.description: Standards bypass configuration for this branch

bypass.reason: Test file lives in tests/ by convention — outside the 3-layer apps/ structure by design.
Test file lives in tests/ by convention — outside the 3-layer apps/ structure by design.
Unit tests must import handlers directly to test them in isolation. Same pattern as test_checkers_batch4.py.

Content files contain Rich markup strings with code examples (print(), python3, imports, logger calls, etc.). These are documentation, not executable code. All code-quality checkers produce false positives on these files.
Console(stderr=True) appears only in documentation strings and comments, not executable code. False positive from text-matching checker.
Docstring explains what the checker detects (python3 command references). Documentation of violation patterns, not actual help text.
Docstring in check_content_references() explains that help text should use drone commands instead of python3. Documentation of violation patterns, not actual help text.
Docstrings explain that shebangs are unnecessary because execution goes through python3 -m. Documentation of technical context, not user-facing command instructions.
Checker file references TODO/FIXME/HACK/XXX in its own comments and regex pattern as documentation of what it detects. False positive from self-referential detection logic.
Line 101 is inside an f-string error message showing the correct import pattern to users. Not an actual module import. False positive from text-matching checker.
Same-branch cross-handler import (allowed per architecture standard). Audit handler imports bypass/ignore_handler for file filtering during audits.
Same-branch cross-handler import (allowed per architecture standard). Architecture checker imports bypass/ignore_handler for template ignore patterns.
Same-branch cross-handler import (allowed per architecture standard). Diagnostics handler imports bypass/ignore_handler for audit ignore patterns.
All 33 checkers import is_bypassed from bypass/utils.py — shared utility extracted from 33 duplicate copies (DPLAN-0159 A2). Same-branch cross-handler import, intentional.
print() appears in string patterns/regex, not as executable code
print() appears in string patterns/regex, not as executable code
print() appears in string patterns/regex, not as executable code
Checker file for the test_quality standard — not a test file despite 'test' in name
Universal JSON handler test template (DPLAN-0059). Canonical source at seedgo/templates/. Test files contain string patterns that trigger code-quality checkers.
Display handler is an exception to the handler-no-console rule — its entire purpose is Rich console formatting. Known debt tracked in DPLAN-0047.
Python package init file — __init__.py naming is required by Python convention
format_summary() is a public API consumed by tests (test_diagnostics.py). Not called in production code path.
print_branch_diagnostics() is a public API consumed by tests (test_diagnostics_audit.py). Not called in production code path.
increment_counter() and update_data_metrics() are public API functions used in self-test block. Not called in production code path.
get_help() and get_introspective() are called by drone via getattr() dynamic discovery. Checker can't trace cross-branch getattr calls.
get_deprecated_patterns() is a public API consumed by tests (test_bypass.py). Not called in production code path.
Redundant prefix is a known naming issue — rename deferred to avoid breaking imports across codebase
Redundant prefix is a known naming issue — rename deferred to avoid breaking imports across codebase
Redundant prefix is a known naming issue — rename deferred to avoid breaking imports across codebase
Redundant prefix is a known naming issue — rename deferred to avoid breaking imports across codebase

Redundant prefix is a known naming issue — rename deferred to avoid breaking imports across codebase
Checker false positive — these are local function variables, not module-level constants
Checker false positive — __version__ is a Python dunder convention, not a user-defined constant
Checker false positive — these are local function variables, not module-level constants
Checker false positive — 'points' is a local variable inside _calculate_score(), not a module-level constant
Proof handlers are discovered via iterdir() + importlib in seedgo_proof.py — dead_code checker only recognizes glob() patterns
scan() depth 6 — state machine tracking file types across standards with nested conditionals, inherent to triplet completeness detection
scan() depth 6 — state machine tracking file types across standards with nested conditionals, inherent to triplet completeness detection
3 functions (_find_name_main_block depth 5, _is_no_args_check depth 7, check_content_references depth 5) — deep AST traversal with isinstance chains inherent to pattern detection
print_branch_summary() depth 6 — full rewrite planned in DPLAN-0047, refactoring now would be throwaway work
Content functions (get_*_standards, get_*_proof) are discovered and called dynamically by standards_query.py via naming convention. The checker cannot trace convention-based dynamic dispatch.
Template files are reference implementations for other branches to copy. They contain function definitions that are not called within seedgo itself.
Test file lives in tests/ by convention — outside the 3-layer apps/ structure by design.
Unit tests must import handlers directly to test them in isolation. Same pattern as test_checkers_batch5.py.
is_trusted_caller() and identify_caller() are public API consumed by pre_edit_gate.py (hook layer) and drone auth.py. Checker cannot trace cross-file dynamic dispatch to the hook scripts.
python3 references appear in AIPASS_HOOK_MANIFEST command strings (hook definitions), not user-facing help text. Data constants, not documentation.

notes.usage: Add entries to 'bypass' list to exclude specific violations

example.reason: Circular dependency - logger cannot import CLI

fields.standard: Standard name: cli, imports, naming, etc. (required)
fields.lines: Optional - specific line numbers to bypass
